description:
## Problem

The accessibility dimension in multi-dimensional health scoring penalizes ALL web component libraries because 4 of 8 checks measure data the CEM standard doesn't capture. Cross-library validation against 9 major libraries shows:

- hasAriaRole (20pts): 0/36 pass ‚Äî CEM never carries role info
- hasAriaAttributes (15pts): 0/36 pass ‚Äî CEM never carries aria-* template bindings
- hasFormAssociation (10pts): 0/36 pass ‚Äî Only helix uses formAssociated/internals naming
- hasKeyboardEvents (10pts): 0/36 pass ‚Äî CEM never carries keyboard handlers

That's 55/100 points allocated to checks with 0% pass rate. Every library scores F.

Meanwhile, checks that DO work:
- hasDisabled (15pts): 63.9% pass ‚Äî reliable CEM signal
- hasLabelSupport (10pts): 36.1% pass ‚Äî reliable CEM signal
- hasFocusMethod (15pts): 2.8% pass ‚Äî too narrow (only matches exact `focus()`, misses `setFocus()`)
- accessibilityDescription (5pts): 11.1% pass ‚Äî moderate signal

## Solution

### 1. Reweight dimensions (total still 100):

**Primary signals** (CEM reliably captures these):
- hasDisabled: 20pts ‚Üí 25pts (most reliable a11y signal in CEM)
- hasLabelSupport: 10pts ‚Üí 20pts (second most reliable)
- hasFocusMethod: 15pts ‚Üí 20pts (with broadened detection)
- accessibilityDescription: 5pts ‚Üí 10pts

**Bonus signals** (CEM rarely captures, reward when present):
- hasAriaRole: 20pts ‚Üí 5pts (bonus ‚Äî check tag-name inference too)
- hasAriaAttributes: 15pts ‚Üí 5pts (bonus)
- hasFormAssociation: 10pts ‚Üí 10pts (broadened detection)
- hasKeyboardEvents: 10pts ‚Üí 5pts (with focus/blur event proxy)

### 2. Broaden detection patterns:

**Focus method** ‚Äî Also match:
- `setFocus` (Ionic pattern)
- Any method containing `focus` in name (e.g., `focusInput`, `setFocus`)

**Form association** ‚Äî Also match:
- Components with `name` + `value` + `disabled` fields together (universal form element pattern)
- `form` field/attribute (FAST pattern: member named `form` with attribute `form`)
- Description mentioning "form" association

**Keyboard events** ‚Äî Also match:
- Focus/blur events in the events array (components handling focus likely handle keyboard)
- Events with "key" in the name
- Description mentioning "keyboard" or "key"

**ARIA role** ‚Äî Also infer from tag name:
- `*-button` ‚Üí likely has role=button
- `*-dialog`, `*-modal` ‚Üí likely has role=dialog
- `*-checkbox` ‚Üí likely has role=checkbox
- `*-radio` ‚Üí likely has role=radio
- `*-switch`, `*-toggle` ‚Üí likely has role=switch
- `*-tab`, `*-tabpanel` ‚Üí likely has role=tab
- `*-menu`, `*-menuitem` ‚Üí likely has role=menu
- `*-alert`, `*-toast` ‚Üí likely has role=alert
- `*-combobox`, `*-select` ‚Üí likely has role=combobox
- `*-slider`, `*-range` ‚Üí likely has role=slider
- `*-tooltip` ‚Üí likely has role=tooltip
- `*-tree`, `*-treeitem` ‚Üí likely has role=tree

### Files to modify:

1. `packages/core/src/handlers/accessibility.ts` ‚Äî Reweight, broaden detection, add tag-name heuristics
2. `tests/handlers/accessibility.test.ts` ‚Äî Update test expectations for new weights/detection
3. `tests/handlers/multi-dimensional-health.test.ts` ‚Äî May need score expectation updates

### Acceptance Criteria:

- [ ] No check has 0% pass rate across all 9 fixture libraries
- [ ] A button component with `disabled` + `label` + focus method scores ‚â•50%
- [ ] Tag-name inference correctly identifies common widget roles
- [ ] Form detection catches `name`+`value`+`disabled` pattern
- [ ] Focus detection catches `setFocus()` and focus-related method names
- [ ] All existing tests pass (with updated expectations)
- [ ] New tests cover broadened detection and tag-name heuristics
- [ ] Total weights still sum to 100
title: fix: reweight accessibility analyzer for CEM-realistic scoring
